programs.title: Inside the Factory
programs.desc: The presenters visit the largest factories in the world and reveal the behind-the-scenes of the creation of popular products, normally inaccessible to ordinary consumers.

programs.title: Empire Builders
programs.desc: Rulers of European empires, including the Ottomans, built fortresses around the Mediterranean Sea.These buildings turned out to be later witnesses of powerful battles and the most important events of the millennium.

programs.title: Treasures Decoded
programs.desc: The history of the Sphinx, the Shroud of Turin, the map of El Dorado, and a controversial piece of papyrus.

programs.title: Life Below Zero
programs.desc: Daily struggles of daredevils from various regions of Alaska.The inhabitants of the four houses face further hardships of life in a frosty climate and fight to survive a long and dangerous winter.

programs.title: Planet Earth III
programs.desc:
The third part of the nature documentary, produced over almost five years, concludes the trilogy almost two decades after the first series was broadcast.Its creators used pioneering film technologies, including:light drones, super-fast cameras and remotely controlled deep-sea submarines.In eight episodes, viewers will visit the most remote jungles, the darkest ocean depths, the world's largest caves and the hottest deserts and steppes.They will learn the story about nature's will to survive and the adaptive abilities of various species.The Earth is at a tipping point and changes in the natural world are felt in every ecosystem and affect numerous creatures.The series highlights the growing need to protect and restore nature.In the last episode, viewers will meet inspiring people from various corners of the world who are the face of change and saving the Earth.They are committed to risking their lives to protect the planet.Sir David Attenborough will guide the series
Sir David Attenborough brings you closer to the farthest corners of the globe.Over the course of five years, the British biologist and his team visited 43 countries on six continents.They used technical innovations to show how the planet is changing from different perspectives.The first episode will take viewers, among others, to the infamous Skeleton Coast in Namibia.Here, the oldest desert in the world meets the cold waters of the Atlantic, and hungry lions hunt a colony of seabirds.In the lagoons of Mexican Yucatan, the waters are very salty.Despite unfavorable conditions, Caribbean flamingos appear here.Their offspring will have to survive tropical storms that arrive prematurely.Saving the planet In the next seven installments of the series, filmmakers will take viewers to the most remote jungles, the darkest ocean depths, the world's largest caves and the hottest deserts and steppes.In addition to fascinating stories about the nature that surrounds us, documentarians also raised issues related to its protection.The Earth is at a critical point, and changes in the natural environment are felt in every ecosystem and affect an increasing number of creatures.In the last episode, we will meet inspiring people from different parts of the world who are passionately involved in saving our planet.
